questions.q: In the Ramayana, whose younger brother and inseparable companion is Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is the devoted younger brother of Rama and accompanies him throughout the Ramayana.

questions.q: Who is the father of Lakshmana in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is one of the four sons of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Which queen of Dasharatha is the mother of Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is born to Queen Sumitra, one of the three queens of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the twin brother of Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana and Shatrughna are twin sons of Queen Sumitra.

questions.q: When Rama is exiled to the forest, what does Lakshmana choose to do?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana renounces the comforts of home and follows Rama into the forest exile to serve him and Sita.

questions.q: Whom does Lakshmana help protect and serve during the years of forest exile?
questions.explanation: Throughout the exile, Lakshmana faithfully serves and guards his brother Rama and Rama's wife Sita.

questions.q: Which weapon is most associated with Lakshmana, the warrior brother of Rama?
questions.explanation: Like Rama, Lakshmana is a master archer and is depicted bearing a bow and arrows.

questions.q: In which great epic does Lakshmana appear as a central figure?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is a principal character in the Ramayana, the epic of Rama composed by the sage Valmiki.

questions.q: In the city of Ayodhya, of which kingdom is Lakshmana a prince?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is a prince of the Kosala kingdom, whose capital is Ayodhya, ruled by the Ikshvaku dynasty.

questions.q: Lakshmana is widely revered for which quality in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is celebrated as the ever-faithful brother whose devotion and loyalty to Rama are unwavering.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, Lakshmana is held to be a portion of which cosmic being?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is traditionally regarded as an incarnation of Shesha (Ananta), the cosmic serpent upon whom Vishnu reclines.

questions.q: Who is the wife of Lakshmana, married alongside Sita's wedding to Rama?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is married to Urmila, the sister of Sita and daughter of King Janaka.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, by whose deceptive cry is Lakshmana drawn away from the hermitage, leaving Sita unguarded?
questions.explanation: The demon Maricha, disguised as a golden deer, imitates Rama's voice, prompting Lakshmana to leave Sita and enabling Ravana's abduction of her.

questions.q: Whose nose does Lakshmana cut off in the forest, setting later events in motion in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana cuts off the nose of Surpanakha, Ravana's sister, after she menaces Sita, an act that provokes Ravana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who brings the Sanjivani herb that revives the fallen Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman fetches the Sanjivani herb from a mountain to revive Lakshmana after he is gravely wounded in battle.

questions.q: During the war in Lanka, by whose weapon is Lakshmana struck down and rendered unconscious?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is felled by a powerful weapon hurled by Indrajit, Ravana's son, before being revived by the Sanjivani herb.

questions.q: Which of Ravana's sons does Lakshmana ultimately slay in the battle of Lanka?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana kills Indrajit (Meghanada), Ravana's mighty son, a decisive turning point in the war.

questions.q: From which sacred mountain does Hanuman bring the herb that heals Lakshmana in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman flies to the Himalayan herb-mountain (Dronagiri, near Gandhamadana) to obtain the life-restoring Sanjivani herb for Lakshmana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the four sons of Dasharatha include Rama, Bharata, Shatrughna, and which fourth?
questions.explanation: The four sons of Dasharatha are Rama, Bharata, and the twins Lakshmana and Shatrughna.

questions.q: On the Ramayana journey, with which forest-dwelling king does Lakshmana, alongside Rama, form an alliance?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana accompanies Rama in allying with Sugriva, the vanara king of Kishkindha, who aids in the search for Sita.

questions.q: In many tellings of the Ramayana, what does Lakshmana sacrifice for the years of the forest exile?
questions.explanation: A well-known tradition holds that Lakshmana renounced sleep to keep constant watch over Rama and Sita throughout the fourteen years of forest exile.

questions.q: Which sage takes the young Rama and Lakshmana to protect his sacrifice from demons early in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra leads Rama and Lakshmana to guard his forest sacrifice and trains them in divine weapons.

questions.q: Who is the father of Lakshmana's wife Urmila in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Urmila, the wife of Lakshmana, is a daughter of King Janaka of Mithila and a sister of Sita.

questions.q: According to several traditions, which two sons are born to Lakshmana and Urmila?
questions.explanation: Tradition names Lakshmana and Urmila's sons as Angada and Chandraketu.

questions.q: Which protective boundary, drawn by Lakshmana to guard Sita, appears in many retellings of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: In popular retellings, Lakshmana draws the protective line called the Lakshmana Rekha around the hermitage before leaving Sita.

questions.q: In the Aranya Kanda, which headless, long-armed demon do Rama and Lakshmana slay and liberate from a curse?
questions.explanation: Rama and Lakshmana kill the demon Kabandha, freeing him from a curse, after which he guides them toward Sugriva.

questions.q: Because he was born under Shesha's portion, which epithet relating to his serpent nature is sometimes applied to Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is sometimes called Sheshavatara, since tradition holds him to be an incarnation of the cosmic serpent Shesha.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, while Rama mourns the abducted Sita, what role does Lakshmana most often take?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana repeatedly consoles, steadies, and counsels the grieving Rama, helping renew the search for Sita.

questions.q: Which forest-dwelling chieftain ferries Rama, Lakshmana, and Sita across the Ganga at the start of the exile?
questions.explanation: Guha, the Nishada chieftain and friend of Rama, helps Lakshmana and the others cross the river Ganga into the forest.

questions.q: In the Ramayana's account of Sita's abduction, why is Lakshmana absent when Ravana strikes?
options: He had been sent to follow Rama after the deer's cry
He had gone to seek Sugriva's aid
questions.explanation: Lakshmana leaves the hermitage to follow Rama after the false cry for help, and in his absence Ravana carries off Sita.
